use_codes.desc: REDUCTION OF ATHEROSCLEROTIC EVENTS (MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION, STROKE, AND VASCULAR DEATH) IN PATIENTS WITH ATHEROSCLEROSIS DOCUMENTED BY RECENT STROKE, RECENT MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION OR ESTABLISHED PERIPHERAL ARTERIAL DISEASE

indications.text:
Plavix (clopidogrel bisulfate) is indicated for the reduction of atherothrombotic events as follows:
• Recent MI, Recent Stroke or Established Peripheral Arterial Disease
For patients with a history of recent myocardial infarction (MI), recent stroke, or established
peripheral arterial disease, Plav...

1.1 Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS)
• Plavix is indicated to reduce the rate of myocardial infarction (MI) and stroke in patients with
non–ST-segment elevation ACS (unstable angina [UA]/non–ST-elevation myocardial
infarction [NSTEMI]), including patients who are to be managed medically and those who
a...
